authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is authoritative for the Magnuson-Moss federal warranty baseline, including written warranty disclosure, service-contract boundaries, tie-in-sales prohibitions, dispute procedures, and explicit state-law caveats.

authoritative_sources.title: Dealer's Guide to the Used Car Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC guidance is authoritative for used-vehicle dealer disclosure workflow, Buyers Guide posting, VIN and warranty display requirements, and the boundary between dealer warranty, manufacturer warranty, and service-contract handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: dealer disclosure and used-vehicle warranty posture review

authoritative_sources.title: Early Warning Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA is the primary U.S. motor-vehicle safety regulator. Its EWR guidance is authoritative for manufacturer communications, warranty claims, field reports, and public reporting timeframes that shape the boundary between routine warranty handling and safety escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: warranty-claim categorization and safety-reporting boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Manufacturer Communications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's manufacturer-communications program is authoritative for notices, bulletins, warranty and policy extensions, and product-improvement communications that dealers and warranty operators must not treat as invisible or optional.

authoritative_sources.title: Compendium for EWR for Manufacturers of Motor Vehicles Having an Annual Production of 500 or More Vehicles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's vehicle EWR compendium is an authoritative public operating reference for the definitions of warranty claim, good will, field report, minimal vehicle specificity, and reporting by make, model, model year, and system or component.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: claim evidence normalization and vehicle-specific traceability review

authoritative_sources.title: NHTSA's Product Information Catalog and Vehicle Listing (vPIC)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's VIN and vPIC materials are authoritative public references for VIN decoding and manufacturer-submitted vehicle specifications used to confirm identity and prevent claims from being tied to the wrong vehicle.

authoritative_sources.title: Automotive Service Information Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASTF is a recognized automotive service-information body. Its standards are authoritative public workflow references for OEM service-information access, tooling access, and the response process when service information is incomplete or missing.
used_for: OEM website and tooling retrieval expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Submitting a Service Information Request (SIR)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASTF's SIR workflow is an authoritative public operating reference for what must be attached when service information is missing, including prior OEM contact, VIN, repair description, screenshots, and other supporting evidence.
used_for: proof that OEM contact was attempted before escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Automotive Warranty Management Key Terms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG is a recognized automotive standards body. Its CQI-14 terminology reference is authoritative for public automotive warranty vocabulary such as claim data, labor-op codes, no-trouble-found, product service manual, parts return center, supplier quality, and VIN.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: automotive terminology and returned-part handling review

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for cross-enterprise identifier and traceability exchange. Its global traceability standard is authoritative for identifying, capturing, and sharing traceability data across production, quality, logistics, and aftersales flows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: parts, lot, and cross-system traceability review

workflow_stages.name: repair-order intake and vehicle-grounding review
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant, jurisdiction, OEM, VIN, vehicle family, in-service date, mileage, and accepted repair-order context before the lane advances any warranty or product-service work.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: coverage, bulletin, and service-information prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify warranty terms, Buyers Guide or used-vehicle disclosure posture where relevant, service-contract or CPO context, TSB or policy-extension applicability, prior approvals, and required service information before repair or claim progression.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: repair, claim-line, and parts-evidence progression
workflow_stages.description: Use repair-order detail, DTCs, technician documentation, labor-op data, claim-line support, parts orders, and returned-part instructions to decide whether the case can move into repair, hold, rework, or submission-ready status.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: traceability, quality-hold, and substitution-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile VIN, part, lot, causal-part, inventory, and hold records across DMS, OEM portals, parts-return systems, and quality or supplier notices before closure or reimbursement is recommended.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: exception, safety, and recall-stop escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move repeat repairs, no-trouble-found outcomes, denied claims, open recalls, stop-sales, high-voltage safety concerns, and conflicting policy cases into explicit rework, blocked, or escalated states with correct owner routing.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and closure control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when repair, replacement, denial, or blocked disposition evidence is present and the case remains inside documented warranty-operations authority.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: repair orders, technician stories, diagnostic trouble codes, and test results
VIN decode records, vehicle master data, in-service date, mileage, and warranty-start records
warranty terms, Buyers Guides, service-contract or CPO records, policy extensions, and prior-approval responses
claim lines, labor-op references, authorization numbers, sublet invoices, and OEM portal responses
parts orders, part numbers, causal-part tags, failed-part retention requests, lot or serial evidence, and core-return records
campaign notices, TSBs, recall or stop-sale records, quality holds, and supplier-quality alerts
completion acknowledgements, denial notes, exception summaries, and escalation packages
systems_of_record: VIN decode or vehicle master data service

allowed: classify automotive warranty work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, rework-pending, escalated, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, VIN context, and source context needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: legal interpretation of Magnuson-Moss, state lemon-law, dealer-franchise, or service-contract disputes
final recall, stop-sale, safety, or high-voltage release decisions
goodwill or financial concessions, claim-payment overrides, or reimbursement policy exceptions
engineering root-cause approval, supplier substitution approval, or quality-hold release
refuse: mark warranty work complete without repair-order, claim-line, or required parts and authorization evidence
bypass VIN, mileage, traceability, recall, stop-sale, or parts-retention controls to recover throughput or CSI
treat incomplete or conflicting vehicle, claim, parts, or quality records as acceptable proof of coverage or closure

domain_failure_modes: the wrong VIN, in-service date, or mileage posture drives a claim decision because authoritative vehicle records were not reconciled
a warranty or goodwill case is processed even though the VIN is on an open recall, stop-sale, or safety-critical path
a claim is closed without technician notes, labor-op support, returned-parts evidence, or required authorizations
DMS, OEM portal, and parts-return systems disagree on the causal part, failed-part retention, or claim status
an alternate or held part is used without documented supplier-quality or OEM approval
repeat-repair or no-trouble-found cases are treated as complete rather than rework or escalation problems
the lane drifts into finance, legal, engineering, supplier-approval, or safety-release authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-level lemon-law, implied-warranty, and franchise reimbursement differences
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: As of April 10, 2026, the public FTC baseline does not resolve state-specific lemon-law remedies, implied-warranty limits, or dealer-franchise reimbursement rules. The tenant must supply jurisdiction-specific policy or counsel guidance before those issues determine the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: OEM-specific prior-approval, returned-parts, and EV high-voltage authorization rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public NHTSA, NASTF, AIAG, and GS1 sources establish the automotive baseline, but OEM manuals and tenant controls still determine exact labor-op, prior-approval, failed-part retention, and high-voltage technician authorization requirements for a given case.

notes: Authoritative vehicle, warranty, repair, parts, and campaign records are the DMS, OEM warranty portal, VIN services, parts-return systems, and recall or quality repositories designated by the tenant, constrained by FTC, NHTSA, NASTF, AIAG, and GS1 baselines.

The lane stops before legal remedy interpretation, final recall or stop-sale command, engineering disposition, goodwill approval, supplier substitution approval, and high-voltage safety release even when the service queue is under pressure.
